score_rationale: No evidence was found regarding SecuraOps' management of cybersecurity risks related to its own third-party suppliers or subcontractors.
gaps: No documented process for identifying or assessing third-party suppliers; No evidence of risk management activities (e.g., due diligence, monitoring, or reviews) for subcontractors; No mention of contractual security requirements for suppliers
recommendation: Implement a Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) program to identify, assess, and monitor cybersecurity risks associated with SecuraOps' suppliers and subcontractors.

score_rationale: No evidence of explicit security clauses in contracts with SecuraOps' own subcontractors or third-party suppliers was found in the provided graph evidence.
gaps: No explicit security clauses in contracts with subcontractors; No documented right-to-audit or security review requirements for subcontractors; No evidence of contractual obligations related to security for Tier-N suppliers
recommendation: Include explicit security clauses, right-to-audit provisions, and security review requirements in all contracts with subcontractors and third-party suppliers.

score_rationale: Evidence shows TLS 1.3 is used for communications and encryption, but lacks confirmation of Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) activation and HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) implementation.
gaps: No evidence of Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) activation; No evidence of HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) implementation; No technical details confirming enforcement of TLS 1.3 as minimum version
recommendation: Provide documented evidence of PFS activation, HSTS implementation, and explicit confirmation of TLS 1.3 as the minimum enforced version.

score_rationale: Evidence shows encryption at rest is implemented (AES-256, BitLocker, WORM) and key management (Azure Key Vault), but scope (e.g., production-only or all data) is not explicitly defined.
gaps: Scope of encryption at rest (all data, production-only, or partial) not specified
recommendation: Document the explicit scope of encryption at rest (e.g., all data or production-only) to fully meet the control requirements.

score_rationale: No technical evidence of SPF, DKIM, or DMARC configuration for SecuraOps' email domains was found in the provided graph evidence.
gaps: Missing SPF record for SecuraOps' email domain; Missing DKIM configuration for SecuraOps' email domain; Missing DMARC policy for SecuraOps' email domain; No evidence of alignment parameters or reporting addresses
recommendation: Publish explicit SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for SecuraOps' email domains in DNS, including alignment parameters and reporting addresses.

score_rationale: Evidence shows the presence of an EDR solution for workstations and incident detection, but lacks details on centralized management, firewall activation, or coverage for servers.
gaps: No evidence of centralized management of protections; No mention of firewall activation on endpoints; No evidence of EDR or antivirus coverage for servers
recommendation: Document and provide evidence of centralized management, firewall activation on all endpoints, and protection coverage for servers.

score_rationale: No evidence related to full-disk encryption (FDE) on workstations, deployment scope, or key management was found in the provided graph.

recommendation: Provide documented evidence of FDE implementation on workstations, including deployment scope and key recovery procedures.

score_rationale: Partial evidence of hardening practices exists (CISO validation for exposed components), but no comprehensive baseline, standards, or scope coverage is documented.
gaps: No documented baseline of secure configuration; No reference to hardening standards (CIS, vendor baselines); No evidence of admin rights restriction, device control, or application allowlisting/denylisting; No defined scope (workstations, servers, or all systems)
recommendation: Document a hardening baseline aligned with industry standards (e.g., CIS) and define scope, admin rights restrictions, and device/application controls.

score_rationale: Evidence shows multiple security-related policies with identified owners and versioning, but no explicit Information Security Policy (ISP) document or documented review cycle is identified.

recommendation: Develop and formally document an overarching Information Security Policy (ISP) with a defined review cycle (e.g., annual review) and ensure it is approved by the CISO or IT Director.

score_rationale: Evidence shows a designated CISO role with formal responsibilities and hierarchical linkage, but lacks documented review cycle or explicit validation of responsibilities.

score_rationale: Evidence shows structured risk identification and mitigation protocols, but lacks formal documentation of a risk analysis process, frequency, methodology, or a risk register.

score_rationale: No valid evidence of MFA implementation for internal users (administrators or standard users) was found in the provided graph data.
gaps: No documented MFA policy or implementation for internal users; No evidence of MFA coverage scope (admin-only, all users, etc.); No evidence of MFA method robustness (e.g., factors used)

score_rationale: Evidence of a Joiner-Mover-Leaver (JML) process exists but lacks completeness, particularly in automation and rapidity of deprovisioning during leaver events.
gaps: No evidence of automated provisioning/deprovisioning for identities; No documented timeline for rapid deactivation of accounts during leaver events; Missing review cycle or validation step for JML process effectiveness
recommendation: Implement automated provisioning/deprovisioning tools and define a maximum timeline (e.g., 24 hours) for account deactivation during leaver events.

score_rationale: Evidence of a PAM solution and session recording exists, but scope, review cycles, and emergency access mechanisms are not fully documented or verified.
gaps: No documented review cycle for privileged access; No evidence of a break-glass (emergency access) mechanism; Scope of PAM solution not fully defined (e.g., coverage of all critical systems)
recommendation: Document and implement a break-glass procedure and establish a regular review cycle for privileged access logs and accounts.
